originalTitle: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
title: Helping Our Friends in Sudan: A Story of Hope and Peace
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm your friendly news reporter for Africa, here to tell you about something very important happening in a country called Sudan. Sudan is a beautiful country in Africa, just like ours, full of amazing people, sunshine, and stories.

What is Happening in Sudan?

Sometimes, grown-ups in Sudan are having a very big disagreement. It's like when friends argue, but much, much bigger and very sad. These grown-up fights are called conflict, and they are making many people feel scared and unsafe. Because of these big arguments, some families have to leave their homes. Imagine having to leave your house, your toys, and your cozy bed behind. It's very difficult for them. They might not have enough food or clean water, and it's hard for children to go to school. Their hearts must feel very heavy with worry. **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries, each with its own special cultures, languages, and beautiful places!

Hearts are Hurting and Scared

The fights are causing a lot of sadness and fear. The grown-ups who are fighting are sometimes doing very hurtful things to other people. These hurtful things are not fair. They are happening to make people feel very, very scared, so scared that they can't live their normal lives or feel safe even in their own towns. The United Nations, which we call the UN for short, is like a big team of grown-ups from all over the world. They watch what is happening and try to help. The UN says that these very scary and hurtful things are happening more and more often. It makes many hearts ache to hear that people, including children, are feeling so unsafe and scared. Everyone deserves to feel safe and loved, especially children. **Did you know?** The United Nations (UN) is like a big club of countries that work together to make the world a better, safer, and fairer place for everyone, especially children!

Who is Helping Our Friends?

The UN is trying very hard to help. They are asking the grown-ups who are fighting to stop. They want everyone to talk and find a peaceful way to solve their problems. Many kind people, like doctors and helpers from different countries, are also working in Sudan. They are bringing food, medicine, and clean water to families who need it. They are also trying to make safe places for children and families to stay, where they don't have to worry about the fighting. These helpers are like real-life superheroes, bringing kindness and care to people who are having a very tough time. They show us that even when things are sad, there is always hope and people who care. **Did you know?** When people fight, it can break houses and make it very hard for shops to get food. That's why peace is so important!

Wishing for Peace and Smiles

What we want for our friends in Sudan is peace. Peace means everyone can be safe, go to school, play with their friends, and live happily in their homes. Peace means no more fighting, no more fear, and lots of smiles. It means children can dream big dreams and grow up strong and happy. We hope that the grown-ups who are fighting will listen to the UN and choose peace. We hope they will remember that everyone deserves to feel safe and loved. Let's send our kind thoughts and wishes to the children and families in Sudan. We hope that sunshine and happiness will return to their lives very soon. **Did you know?** Every child in the world, no matter where they live, deserves to feel safe, loved, and have a chance to learn and play! We believe that with the help of kind people and the UN, peace will come back to Sudan. And when peace comes, all the sad hearts can start to heal, and everyone can play and laugh under the bright African sun once again. **Did you know?** Sharing kindness, even with a smile or a helping hand, helps make our whole world a much brighter and happier place for everyone!
summary: Grown-ups in Sudan are fighting, making many people, especially children, feel scared and unsafe, but the United Nations and other helpers are working hard to bring peace and safety back to the country.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone, especially children, deserves to live in a safe place, go to school, and feel loved, and we need to help our friends in Sudan find peace and happiness again.

title: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body
content: Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remained in dispute 12 months after he died.

originalTitle: Israel’s deliberate targeting of Palestinian children
title: Helping Children in Faraway Lands: Why Peace Matters!
content:
Hello, Friends! From Your African Reporter!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly reporter from Africa here! I hope you are all having a super day, learning new things and playing lots of fun games. Today, I want to talk about something important that helps us understand the world a little better. We're going to talk about children in a place far away, and why it's so important for everyone to live in peace. Here in Africa, we know that children love to laugh, play, and learn. It's the same for children everywhere, all around the big wide world! Every child deserves to feel safe and happy, to go to school, and to play with their friends without any worries. Don't you agree?

Children All Around the World

Imagine you are at school right now. Maybe you are learning about numbers, or drawing a beautiful picture. Perhaps you are playing tag in the playground with your best friends. These are all wonderful things that make up a happy childhood. Every child, no matter where they live, wants to do these things. Did you know? There are over 2 billion children in the world! That's a super big number, like counting all the stars in the sky, almost! Each one of them is special and important. Children in every country dream of growing up healthy and strong. They want to learn and make new friends. They want their homes to be safe and full of love. This is why we care about what happens to children everywhere, even in places we might not know much about yet.

What is Happening Far Away?

Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries have big disagreements. When these disagreements get very, very big, they can cause trouble, and sometimes even fighting. This fighting is sometimes called a 'conflict' or 'war.' It's a very sad thing, because it can hurt many people, especially children. There are places far away in the world where children are living through these difficult times. Two of these places are called Gaza and the West Bank. They are parts of a land called Palestine. The children there are like you – they love to play, they want to go to school, and they need to feel safe. But because of the fighting and grown-up problems between countries like Israel and Palestine, their lives can be very hard. Their homes might not be safe anymore, and sometimes it's hard for them to go to school or even play outside.

Who Are the Children in Palestine?

The children in Palestine are brave boys and girls who live in places like cities, towns, and villages. They have families who love them very much, just like your family loves you. They have their own games, their own special foods, and their own dreams. Imagine if your playground suddenly wasn't safe, or if your school had to close. That's what some children in Gaza and the West Bank experience. It’s not fair, because all children should have the chance to be safe and happy. Did you know? The olive tree is a very special tree in Palestine. It can live for hundreds of years and is a symbol of peace and hope for many people there.

Big People Try to Help: The United Nations

Luckily, there are many good people and big groups who try to help. One very important group is called the United Nations, or the UN for short. The UN is like a super big club for almost all the countries in the world! Their main job is to try and make the world a peaceful place. They also try to help people who are in trouble, especially children. They have special teams that go to places where there is fighting to try and make things better. Did you know? The United Nations was started many years ago, after a big world war, because people wanted to make sure countries could talk instead of fight. They work very hard for peace.

What the Report Says About Children

Recently, the United Nations wrote a special report. A 'report' is like a very long story or a big book of facts. This report was about the children in Gaza and the West Bank. It shared important information about how the fighting and other big problems are affecting their lives. It said that because of the troubles, many children have been hurt. Some children have lost their homes, and others have found it very difficult to go to school. Imagine trying to learn your ABCs or 123s when you are worried about your home or your family! It would be very hard. This report helps everyone around the world understand how much children are suffering. It tells us that many things that make children happy and safe are being taken away because of the fighting. This is why the UN wants everyone to know so they can help.

Why Children Need Our Help

Every child has rights. These are like special rules that say what every child should have. One important right is the right to be safe. Another is the right to go to school and learn. And of course, the right to play and have fun! When there is fighting, these rights can be broken. Children might not be safe. They might not be able to go to school. They might not be able to play. This is why it's so important for big people, and even for us, to think about these children. Did you know? The 'Convention on the Rights of the Child' is a special agreement that almost all countries in the world have signed. It lists all the rights children have, like the right to food, health, education, and protection.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still do good things! We can learn about what is happening in the world. We can talk to our parents and teachers about it. And we can always hope and wish for peace. Wishing for peace means wishing for the fighting to stop, and for all children to be safe and happy again. It means wishing for grown-ups to find ways to agree without fighting. We can also remember to be kind to everyone we meet, no matter where they come from. Kindness can spread like sunshine and help make the world a better place, one smile at a time.

Let's Hope for Happy Times!

So, my dear friends, remember the children in Gaza and the West Bank. Remember that they are just like you, wanting to play, learn, and be safe. Let's hope that the grown-ups can find a way to bring peace to their homes very soon. Let's all work towards a world where every child, everywhere, can grow up with joy, laughter, and endless fun. Because every child deserves a happy childhood. Thank you for listening, and until next time, keep shining bright! Did you know? The symbol for peace is often a white dove or an olive branch, which both represent hope and calm. Peace is when everyone can live together without fighting.
summary: A UN report shares sad news about how fighting in faraway places like Gaza and the West Bank is hurting children there, making it hard for them to be safe, go to school, or play, and reminding us all that children everywhere deserve peace.
whyMatters: This is important because every child in the world deserves to be safe and happy, to go to school, and to play, and when there is fighting, children get hurt and lose these important things, so we must care and hope for peace for them.

content: England and Ghana share a 0-0 draw in Group L, despite a late barrage by the English.

originalTitle: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
title: Helping Sailors and Sharing the Big Blue Sea!
content:
Hello, my friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter from sunny Africa! I'm here to tell you about some important news happening far away, but it's super interesting, especially if you love big ships and helping people. Get ready for a story about sailors, a special water road, and why sharing is always best!

A Special Water Road: The Strait of Hormuz

Imagine you have a big garden, and there's a small, important gate that helps you go from one part of the garden to another. This gate is like a very special water path in the world. It's called the Strait of Hormuz. It's not just any water path; it's like a super highway for ships! It connects two very big parts of the sea. Many, many ships travel through this narrow water road every single day. **Did you know?** A "strait" is a skinny part of the sea that connects two bigger parts of the sea. It's like a watery bridge!

Why This Water Road is So Important

Why is this water road so important, you ask? Well, these big ships carry amazing things! They carry oil, which helps our cars move and keeps the lights on in our houses. They also carry food to eat, clothes to wear, and even some of your favorite toys! Imagine if these things couldn't travel easily. It would be hard to get all the yummy fruits from other countries or the cool new school supplies. So, this water road helps everyone around the world get what they need.

Sailors Need Our Help!

Now, sometimes, things don't go as planned. Sailors are very brave people who work on these big ships. They spend many days and nights on the sea, bringing us important things. But guess what? Some sailors got stuck! They are "stranded," which means their ships can't move, and they are stuck far from home. Imagine your school bus breaking down in the middle of a trip. You would need help, right? These sailors really need a helping hand to get back safely.

Who is the UN? Our Big Helper Friends!

Here comes the good news! There is a very, very big team that helps people all over the world. It's called the United Nations, or we can just say the UN. The UN is like a super big club for almost all the countries in the whole world. Their most important job is to help people, keep everyone safe, and make sure countries are friends. **Did you know?** The UN does amazing things like helping children go to school, making sure people have enough food to eat, and sending doctors to help sick people in many places, even here in Africa!

The UN to the Rescue!

So, when the UN heard about the sailors who were stuck, they quickly said, "Don't worry, we will help you!" They want to make sure these brave sailors are safe and can go back home to their families. The UN is now making plans to rescue these sailors from their ships. This is wonderful news for the sailors and for everyone who cares about them. It shows that when we work together, we can help people in need.

A Warning About "Sea Tolls"

Now, there's another part of this story about the special water road. A very important leader from a country called America, his name is Mr. Marco Rubio, said something important. He told another country, Iran, that they should not ask for money from ships that want to use this water road. This money is sometimes called a "toll" or a "fee." Imagine if you had to pay money every time you wanted to walk on a public path in your village. That wouldn't be fair, right?

Why No Tolls on This Water Road?

Most countries believe that the Strait of Hormuz, this special water road, should be free for all ships. It's like a public road that everyone can use without paying. Why is it important for it to be free? Because it helps everything move smoothly. If ships have to pay extra money, then the food, clothes, and toys they carry might cost more for us to buy. **Did you know?** Many important parts of the ocean that connect countries are called "international waters." This means they belong to no one country and are free for all ships to use.

Sharing the Sea and Being Friends

So, Mr. Rubio is reminding everyone that we should share the sea. Just like it's good to share toys with your friends, it's good for countries to share these important water paths. It helps everyone around the world. When countries work together and follow the rules, it makes trade easier and helps people get the things they need more cheaply.

What Happens Next?

The UN will keep working hard to make sure all the stranded sailors get home safely. That's the most important thing right now. And leaders from different countries will keep talking to make sure everyone understands the rules about using the sea paths. They want to make sure ships can always travel freely and safely. It's all about helping people who are stuck and making sure that the important things we use every day can keep moving around the world without problems.

Why This Story Matters to Us

This news might seem like it's happening very far away from our homes in Africa. But guess what? It touches us too! Many of the things we use and enjoy every day come to us on those big ships. Things like medicines to keep us healthy, parts for our radios, or even some delicious snacks from other countries! When these sea roads are safe and free for all ships, it helps everyone, including us right here. It means more good things can come to our homes and help our families. So, this story is about helping people, sharing, and making sure our big, beautiful world works together smoothly. Keep smiling, my friends!
summary: The United Nations is helping sailors who are stuck in a special sea path called the Strait of Hormuz, while a US leader, Marco Rubio, warns that no country should charge money for ships to use this important water road.
whyMatters: This is important because safe and free sea paths help ships carry food, oil, and other things we use every day to our homes in Africa and all around the world, making sure everyone gets what they need.
